newsBriefing: The sports world witnessed a blend of historic milestones and somber triumphs today, headlined by Lionel Messi reaching his 900th career goal even as Inter Miami suffered a surprising Champions Cup elimination. In the basketball ranks, injury woes dominated the narrative as Louisville’s star freshman Mikel Brown Jr. was ruled out of the NCAA Tournament opener and SMU coach Andy Enfield defended his team's transparency regarding B.J. Edwards' health following their First Four exit. Professional stars also clarified their futures, with LeBron James withdrawing interest from a Las Vegas expansion bid and Steve Kerr conceding the Golden State Warriors are firmly bound for the play-in tournament. On the ice, the Blackstone Valley Co-op hockey team delivered the day’s most emotional story, securing a Rhode Island state title in quadruple overtime led by captain Colin Dorgan, who lost three family members in a recent rink shooting. Meanwhile, institutional shifts loom on the horizon as the Cleveland Browns proposed extending the NFL's trade window for draft picks to five years, and MLB Commissioner Rob Manfred teased a potential move for the World Baseball Classic to midseason. Amidst these changes, the return of New York Yankees ace Gerrit Cole to the mound for his first spring training start since surgery offered a promising glimpse into the upcoming baseball season.
